chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI CP No.D-4892 of 2024 (Ghulam Mustafa v. Government of Sindh and others) ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- DATE: ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF THE JUDGE 1. For orders on Misc. No.21729/2024 2. For orders on Misc. No.21730/2024 3. For hearing of main case 04.10.2024 Mr. Muhammad Umar Panhwar, advocate for the petitioner.-.-.-.-.-.-. 1. Urgency granted. 2-3. It is a robbery case, the FIR whereof, the petitioner has sought to be quashed by this Court by way of the instant petition. The FIR was registered on 10.06.2014, the prescribed period for submission of the charge sheet as per proviso (i) to Section 173 Cr.CPC is 14 days, it might have been submitted before the Magistrate having jurisdiction. If for the sake of arguments, it is believed that it has not yet been done even then such an FIR involving the issue of robbery could hardly be quashed by this Court mainly for the reason that it does not contain the name of the petitioner. Consequently, the instant petition being misconceived is dismissed in limine, directing the petitioner to prove his innocence by joining the investigation/trial as the case may be. JUDGE JUDGE Nadir*
